---
title: "Analysis"
#Key Takeaways
Long-Term Comparative Scope: This study analyzes the quantitative relationship between the USD/TRY exchange rate and Borsa Istanbul (BIST) indices across a 20-year span (2006–2026), utilizing 5-year analysis windows to identify structural shifts in market behavior.

The 2011–2020 Depreciation Cycle: During the 2011–2015 and 2016–2020 periods, significant currency depreciation (as shown by the USD/TRY parity doubling and tripling) was generally correlated with negative USD-denominated returns, with the Banking sector consistently being the most vulnerable to exchange rate shocks.

Structural Divergence (2021–2026): In a historical anomaly, the most recent period (2021–2026) shows that despite the USD/TRY parity increasing six-fold (from 100 to over 600), BIST indices provided a robust hedge against hyper-depreciation, yielding positive USD-denominated returns for the first time in a decade.

Sectoral Sensitivity and Resilience: While the Industrial index (XUSIN.IS) typically offered more stability across all cycles, the Banking sector (XBANK.IS) displayed extreme volatility but showed a surprising recovery in USD terms during the 2021–2026 period.

Investment Implications: The analysis concludes that while exchange rate spikes historically triggered market sell-offs, the current economic regime has transformed the Turkish equity market into a primary vehicle for capital preservation against extreme currency volatility.

#Correlation Matrix (full period)
cat("\n?????? Full-Period Log-Return Correlation Matrix ??????\n")
cor_mat <- cor(return_data[, ret_cols], use = "complete.obs")
rownames(cor_mat) <- colnames(cor_mat) <- gsub("^Ret_", "", rownames(cor_mat))
print(round(cor_mat, 4))

usd_cor_row <- grep("USD.TRY", rownames(cor_mat), value = TRUE)[1]
cat("\n>> USD/TRY correlations with BIST indices:\n")
usd_cors <- sort(cor_mat[usd_cor_row, ], decreasing = TRUE)
print(round(usd_cors[names(usd_cors) != usd_cor_row], 4))
  The matrix below shows the overall 20-year correlation between USD/TRY returns and BIST indices.

# Period-Based Analysis

::: {.panel-tabset}

## 2006-2010
Below are the visual insights for the first analysis window:

![Cumulative % Growth (2006-2010)](grafik1.png)
![Daily Volatility & Shocks (2006-2010)](grafik2.png)
![USD/TRY vs BIST Scatter (2006-2010)](grafik3.png)
![USD-Denominated BIST vs USD/TRY Parity (2006-2010)](grafik4.png)

## 2011-2015
Analysis of the second 5-year period:

![Cumulative % Growth (2011-2015)](grafik5.png)
![Daily Volatility & Shocks (2011-2015)](grafik6.png)
![USD/TRY vs BIST Scatter (2011-2015)](grafik7.png)
![USD-Denominated BIST vs USD/TRY Parity (2011-2015)](grafik8.png)

## 2016-2020
Market dynamics during the currency shocks of late 2010s:

![Cumulative % Growth (2016-2020)](grafik9.png)
![Daily Volatility & Shocks (2016-2020)](grafik10.png)
![USD/TRY vs BIST Scatter (2016-2020)](grafik11.png)
![USD-Denominated BIST vs USD/TRY Parity (2016-2020)](grafik12.png)

## 2021-Present
Recent structural shifts and capital preservation trends:

![Cumulative % Growth (2021-2026)](grafik13.png)
![Daily Volatility & Shocks (2021-2026)](grafik14.png)
![USD/TRY vs BIST Scatter (2021-2026)](grafik15.png)
![USD-Denominated BIST vs USD/TRY Parity (2021-2026)](grafik16.png)

:::





---
